steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: When a transversal crosses two lines, equal corresponding angles are sufficient to prove the two lines are parallel. The diagram shows $w$ and $y$ as corresponding angles.

steps.title: Match the two angles
steps.content: Given $w=146$, the statement $y=146$ would make $w$ and $y$ equal. Equal corresponding angles prove that lines $r$ and $s$ are parallel. The answer is B.
